original: Origin of Dionysian Incarnation of the Word
The first Easter is celebrated by Christians, according to calendar-maker Dionysius Exiguus
City of Venice is founded, at noon on a Friday
Constantine begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Tichborne family of Hampshire England start tradition of giving gallon of flour to residents to keep deathbed promise
Consecration of the Scrovegni Chapel (Arena Chapel) in Padua, Italy, with fresco masterpiece by Florentine painter Giotto
Council of Pisa opens - elects Antipope Alexander V
7th Spanish India Armada of 21 ships led by Francisco de Almeida, departs Lisbon, aiming to establish a Portuguese Viceroy in India
Catholic Italian businessman Roberto Ridolfi leaves England
Recantation of Loos, Dutch scholar Coinelius Loos recants his earlier written protest against witchcraft persecution in Trier, Germany before officials in Brussels
Under charter granted to Lord Baltimore and led by his brother Leonard Calvert first settlers found Catholic colony of Maryland
Dutch East India Company ship Nieuw Haarlem is shipwrecked without loss of life in shallow waters in Table Bay, Cape of Good Hope, leading to the founding of the Dutch Cape Colony
Mount Etna in Sicily erupts, destroying Nicolosi, killing 20,000
Great Britain and the French Republic sign the Treaty of Amiens
First fare-paying, passenger railway service in the world established on the Oystermouth Railway in Swansea, Wales
1st US flag flown in battle on the Pacific by the frigate Essex
Netherlands Bank established
Tsar Alexander Irecommends formation of Society of Israeli Christians
The Greek revolution against the Ottoman Turks is officially declared, with hostilities having started two months earlier
Pope Pius issues IX encyclical "On aid for Ireland"
Yosemite Valley discovery made public by Major James D. Savage and Captain John Boling after being shown by Indian guides in California
Friedrich Hebbel's tragedy "Agnes Bernauer" premieres in Munich
A E Burnside patents Burnside carbine
Édouard-Léon Scott de Martinville receives a patent for his phonautograph, a device which created visual images of sound
Skirmish at Brentwood, Tennessee
Battle of Paducah, Kentucky (Forrest's raid)
Battle of Bluff Spring, Florida
Battle of Mobile, Alabama (Spanish Fort, Fort Morgan, Fort Blakely)
Battle of Petersburg: Confederate forces launch an unsuccessful counterattack on Fort Stedman
SS General Lyon at Cape Hatteras catches fire & sinks, killing 400
Glasgow 1st soccer match Scotland defeat Wales (4-0)
1st demonstration of pancake making, held at a department store in NYC
Socialist leader Domela Nieuwenhuis elected to Dutch 2nd Chamber
1st Test Cricket match played at Newlands, Cape Town v England
Coxey's Army of the unemployed sets out from Massillon, Ohio, for Washington, D.C.
Italian troops invade Abyssinia (Ethiopia)
Intercollegiate Trapshooting Association formed in NYC
Swami Vivekananda initiates Margaret Noble (Sister Nivedita) into the vow of Brahmacharya, the first Western woman received into an Indian monastic order
US Socialist Party forms in Indianapolis
55 die as Rock Island train derailed near Marshalltown, Iowa
In Russia, 567 students are tried for rioting and 'political disaffection' are found guilty; 95 are banished to Siberia
Irving W. Colburn patents sheet glass drawing machine
Racing Club de Avellaneda, one of the big five of Argentina, is founded.
Confederate battle flags captured during the American Civil War are returned to South
Stanley Cup, Winnipeg Auditorium, Winnipeg, Manitoba: Montreal Wanderers lose to Kenora Thistles, 6-5 but win on 2 game aggregate, 12-8
Clube Atletico Mineiro, Founded in Belo Horizonte, Brazil
Chalmers Auto Co offers a new car to each leagues' batting champ
L. D. Swamikannu publishes "Manual of Indian Chronology" in Bombay
Triangle Shirtwaist Factory catches fire 145 die, all but 13 girls in Greenwich Village, New York
Great Dayton Flood: winter rains cause Great Miami River to flood, Ohio's greatest natural disaster
Home of vaudeville, Palace Theatre, opens (NYC) starring Ed Wynn
1st submarine disaster; a US F-4 sinks off Hawaii, killing 21
German U boat torpedoes Netherlands merchant ship Medea
Jess Willard fights Frank Moran to no decision in 10 for heavyweight boxing title in NYC
Belarusian People's Republic is established
Greek Independence Day
British government grants Trans-Jordan autonomy
Greek parliament selects admiral Paul Koundouriotis as premier
Stanley Cup Final, Ottawa Auditorium, Ottawa, ON: Montreal Canadiens (NHL) beat Calgary Tigers (WCHL), 3-0 for a 2-0 series sweep
Hal Kemp & his Orchestra record "Whistles", with Skinnay Ennis as vocalist, in NYC
Scottsboro Boys arrested in Alabama, accused of raping a white woman
1st Belgium government of Van Zealand resigns
200-inch mirror blank to build the Hale telescope leaves Corning New York for California (then largest telescopic mirror ever made)
Italy & Yugoslavia sign non-aggression treaty (Pact of Belgrade)
Lionel Conacher misses on 1st Stanley Cup penalty shot
Washington Daily News is 1st US newspaper with perfumed advertising page
Billboard Magazine introduces the hillbilly (country) music chart
Carolina Paprika Mills in Dillon, South Carolina, incorporated
1st 700 Jews from Polish Lvov district reach the Bełżec Concentration camp
97% of all Dutch physicians strike against Nazi registration
Germany troop executes 335 residents of Rome
US 1st army breaks out bridgehead near Remagen, Germany after crossing the Rhine
US 4th Armored div arrives at Hanau & Aschaffenburg
US Northern Tractor Flotilla departs Ulithi to Okinawa
Agreement of Linggadjati ratified in Batavia
Coal mine explosion in Centralia, Illinois kills 111
Last day of Test cricket for Walter Hammond (v NZ, Christchurch)
The Soviet Union begins Operation Pribioi, the mass deportation of 90,000 Estonians, Latvians and Lithuanians to inhospitable areas in the Soviet Union
5th Tony Awards: "Guys & Dolls" (musical) and "The Rose Tattoo" (play) win
Edward Mills Purcell and Harold I. Ewen detect 21-cm radiation at Harvard physics lab
RCA manufactures the first color TV set, featuring a 12.5-inch screen and costing $1,000
East Germany granted full sovereignty by occupying power, USSR
NBA modifies the free-throw rule
Treaty of Rome establishes European Economic Community (Common Market)
West German parliament desires German atomic weapons
Bill White traded to St Louis for pitchers Sam Jones & Don Choate
French President De Gaulle acknowledges Oder-Neisse boundary
First guided missile launched from a nuclear powered submarine (USS Halibut)
Italian government Tambroni forms
3rd place game is one of the wildest contests in NCAA Tournament history as St Joseph's defeats Utah, 127-120 in quadruple overtime
Explorer 10 launched into elongated Earth orbit (177/181,000 km)
Sputnik 10 carries a dog into Earth orbit; later recovered
French OAS-leader ex-general Jouhaud arrested
KWHY TV channel 22 in Los Angeles, CA (IND) begins broadcasting
Egypt ends state of siege (1952-64)
West German Bondsdag extends war crimes retribution
Beatles pose for photographer Robert Whitaker with mutilated dolls and butchered meat for album cover of "Yesterday & Today", it is later pulled from circulation and replaced with a different photo
US Supreme court rules "poll tax" unconstitutional
The Turtle's "Happy Together" goes #1
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Who & Cream make US debut at Murray the K's Easter Show
KLVX TV channel 10 in Las Vegas, NV (PBS) begins broadcasting
Members of the Derry Housing Action Committee (DHAC) disrupt a meeting of Londonderry Corporation to protest at the lack of housing provision in the city, Northern Ireland
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Andes Pact signed in Peru
Pakistan General Agha Mohammed Jagja Khan succeeds Ayub Chan as president
Concorde makes its 1st supersonic flight (700 MPH/1,127 KPH)
European council accepts Mansholt Plan laying off 5 million farmers
NFL football team Boston Patriots become New England Patriots as they relocate to Foxboro. Massachusetts
America's LP "America" goes #1
UCLA wins its 6th consecutive national basketball title
27th Tony Awards: "That Championship Season" (play) and "A Little Night Music" (musical) win
AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament, Immaculata beats Queens College, 59-52, Immaculata 1st undefeated team in New York
Chipko forest conservation movement begins in Reni Village, Western Himalayas, India, when local woman Gaura Devi hugs a tree to prevent its logging
St Catherine's Monastery on the Sinai peninsula, discovers a cache of manuscript fragments including pages of the Codex Sinaiticus and a palimpsest with the first ever example of Caucasian Albanian
Argentine military junta bans leftist political parties
AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament, UCLA beat Maryland 90-74 in Los Angeles
AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament, Old Dominion beat Louisiana Tech 75-65 in Greensboro
Major riot at Bourda prevents day's play in WSC Supertest
Space Shuttle Columbia arrives at Kennedy Space Centre to prepare for its first launch, which would eventually happen in 1981
1st broadcast of "Cagney & Lacey" starring Sharon Gless and Tyne Daly on CBS-TV
Christa Rothenburger skates world record 500 m ladies (39.69 sec)
Pavel Pegov skates world record 1000m (1:12.58)
Supreme Court rules Air Force could ban wearing of yarmulkes
The US Supreme Court rules that gender-based workplace affirmative action plans do not constitute discrimination on the basis of sex under the Civil Rights Act 1964
NASA launches space vehicle S-206
Fire in illegal NYC social club, kills 87
Nigerian crude becomes competitive in US Gulf Coast as Nigeria cuts crude prices
British scientists find new largest perfect # (2 756839 -1 * 2 756839)
Cosmonaut Sergei Krikalev returns to Earth after a 10-month stay aboard the Mir space station
Russian manned space craft TM-14, lands
Gunda Niemann skates ladies world record 3 km (4:09.32)
Yasunori Miyabe skates world record 1000 m (1:12.37)
BoxerMike Tysonreleased from jail after serving 3 years
Comet C/1996 B2 (Hyakutake) approaches within 0.1018 AUs of Earth
Freedom Shoemakers on Maryport's Solway Estate closes
Ice Dance Championship at Edmonton won by Gritshuk & Platov (RUS)
Ice Pairs Championship at Edmonton won by Eltsova & Bushkov (RUS)
The European Union's Veterinarian Committee bans the export of British beef and its by-products as a result of "mad cow disease" (BSE).
US issues newly-redesigned $100 bill
TV reality show "The Bachelor" hosted by Chris Harrison debuts on ABC in the US
KingRichard I(the Lion Heart) of England, is wounded by a crossbow bolt while fighting France, leads to his death on April 6
Robert the Brucecrowned Robert I, King of Scots, having killed his rival John Comyn, Lord of Badenoch
Portuguese Cortes (King's Court) callsPhilip IIKing of Portugal, further legitimatizing his rule
English explorerWalter Raleighgranted seven-year royal charter byElizabeth Ito explore and colonize North America, after Humphrey Gilbert's death at sea
Cornelis de Houtman's fleet departs Amsterdam for the East Indies (Indonesia) on his second voyage in search of the then exotic spices, cloves, nutmeg, mace, cinnamon and pepper
Henry Hudsonembarks on an attempt to find northwest passage to Asia for the Dutch East India Company - will explore North America instead
AstronomerChristiaan Huygensdiscovers Saturn's largest moon, Titan
Construction begins on evangelistGeorge Whitefield's Bethesda Orphanage in Georgia
Voltaireleaves the court of Frederik II of Prussia
Continental Congress authorizes a medal forGeorge Washington
George Canningbecomes British Foreign Secretary, serving until 1809
Percy Bysshe Shelleyis expelled from the University of Oxford for his publication of the pamphlet "The Necessity of Atheism"
German explorerLudwig Leichhardtarrives in Sydney to a hero's welcome after completing his exploration of Australia's Northern Territory
1st US Army Medal of Honor awarded to six army soldiers by US Secretary of WarEdwin Stantonin Washington
Agostino Depretisbecomes Prime Minister of Italy for the first time as head of a left-leaning government
Canadian flying aceBilly Bishopclaims his first victory, shooting down and mortally wounding German Leutnant Theiller
Woodrow Wilson's dream of a League of Nations becomes a reality after the League Covenant is adopted at the Paris Peace Conference
1st Augusta National Invitation Tournament (Masters) Golf: Horton Smith wins with 20-foot birdie putt at the 17th hole, 1 stroke ahead ofCraig Wood
It is revealed Quaker Oats paysBabe Ruth$25,000 per year for ads
Akira Kurosawa's debut film "Sanshiro Sugata" is released
Jimmy Durante& Garry Moore premiere on radio
1st performance ofIgor Stravinsky's "Ebony Concerto", by Woody Herman and His Thundering Herd, at Carnegie Hall, New York City
26th Academy Awards: "From Here to Eternity" best film,William Holden&Audrey Hepburnbest actor, actress
PopePius XIIencyclical "Sacra virginitas" (On consecrated virginity)
United States Customs seizes copies ofAllen Ginsberg's poem "Howl" as obscene.
Sugar Ray Robinsonis 1st boxing champ to win 5 times
Ford Frickvoids Indians-Red Sox deal as Sam White retires
US Court of Appeal Judge Frederick van Pelt Bryan, in New York City, rules D.H. Lawrence's "Lady Chatterley's Lover" is not obscene, overturning ban imposed by US postal Service

Elvis Presleyperforms live at Pearl Harbor’s Bloch Arena in a benefit for the USS Arizona Memorial; his return to the concert stage after a stint in the US Army raises $60K and worldwide awareness of the project
Britain sets memorial for the late PresidentJohn F. Kennedy
Martin Luther King Jr.leads 25,000 to state capitol in Montgomery, Alabama
American singerJudy Garland’s gives what becomes her very last concert, at Falkoner Centret in Copenhagen, Denmark
BeatleJohn Lennonand new wifeYoko Onostage their 1st bed-in for peace,  at the Hilton Hotel in Amsterdam, Netherlands
Ian Paisleyand Ronald Bunting, loyalists in Northern Ireland are jailed for organising an illegal counter demonstration in Armagh on 30 November 1968
James Callaghanspeaks at a rally of the Northern Ireland labour movement, but rejects calls for the Labour Party to open membership to those living in N. Ireland
Tom Jones' cover version ofPaul Anka's "She's a Lady" goes gold
Chicago Black HawksBobby Hullbecomes the 2nd NHLer to score 600 goals, with a goal in Boston assisted by his brother Dennis
Barbra Streisandrecords the album "Butterfly"
Linda Ronstadtreleases cover of the Everly Brothers' 1960 song "When Will I Be Loved" as a single; climbs to No. 2 in the charts
Czech tennis starMartina Navratilovaretains her WTA Tour Championship with a 6–3, 3–6, 6–2 win over American teenager Tracey Austin in NYC
Wayne Gretzkybecomes 1st NHL to score 200 points in a season

Motown 25, a concert celebrating the anniversary of Motown records is taped for broadcast at the Civic Auditorium in Pasadena, California; performers include: The Miracles; The Temptations; The Four Tops;Diana Rossandthe Supremes; The Jackson 5; andMichael Jackson, who unveils his "moonwalk" dance move
57th Academy Awards: "Amadeus",F. Murray Abraham&Sally Fieldwin
Edwin MeeseIII takes office as US Attorney General
CanadianKurt Browningbecomes the 1st figure skater to land a quadruple jump
"Women on the Verge of a Nervous Breakdown" film written and directed by Pedro Almodóvar, starring Carmen Maura andAntonio Banderasis released
Robin Givensdemands full access to husbandMike Tyson's money
63rd Academy Awards: "Dances with Wolves",Kathy Bates&Jeremy Ironswin
Allan Bordertakes 5-68 v WI at Bourda (!), Georgetown
Country singerReba McEntireperforms as scheduled at the Academy Awards ceremony, just 9 days after nearly her entire band dies in a plane crash
ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Melbourne Cricket Ground, Australia: Pakistan beats England by 22 runs for their first trophy; Player of the Match:Wasim Akram(PAK) 33 (18) & 3/49
68th Academy Awards: "Braveheart",Nicolas Cage&Susan Sarandonwin
"Life After Death" second studio album byThe Notorious B.I.G.is released (Billboard Song of the Year 1997)
MLB Cleveland Indians trade Lofton & Embree to Braves for Marquis Grissom &David Justice
73rd Academy Awards: "Gladiator",Russell Crowe&Julia Robertswin
Capitol/Nashville Records releases "Rules of Travel", the tenth studio album by singer-songwriterRosanne Cash; produced by husband John Leventhal, it includes a duet with her fatherJohnny Cash

Florence Cathedral (Cattedrale di Santa Maria del Fiore), started in 1296, is consecrated by Pope Eugene IV. Designed by Filippo Brunelleschi with support fromCosimo de' Medici, it features the largest dome in the world at the time.
British Parliament abolishes the slave trade throughout the British Empire; a penalty of £120 per slave is introduced for ship captains

WriterO. Henrysentenced to 5 years in prison for embezzling $854 from a bank reportedly to pay for his sick wife's medical bills. Goes on to write many classics while in jail including "Whistling Dick's Christmas Stocking".
